heading: Excise Duty (Amendment) (No. 2) Act, 2023 (Act 1108) — First Schedule substituted

metadata.publisher: Ghana Publishing Company Limited (Assembly Press), Accra
metadata.source_authority: Parliament of the Republic of Ghana
metadata.source_note: Excise Duty (Amendment) (No. 2) Act, 2023 (Act 1108) from the Parliament of Ghana DSpace ORIGINAL bitstream, downloaded 2026-07-07 (scanned print, OCR at 300 dpi). The single section substitutes the whole First Schedule of the Excise Duty Act, 2014 (Act 878); the captured window carries the complete substituted schedule (print pages 3-5). Being a wholesale substitution, this text supersedes the earlier First Schedule chain (Acts 903, 941, 1093 and predecessors) and is the rate table in force for the 2024-2026 years: no later Excise Duty (Amendment) Act exists as of 2026-07-07 (the March and December 2025 tax packages contain none).
metadata.title: Excise Duty (Amendment) (No. 2) Act, 2023 (Act 1108) — First Schedule substituted

citation_label: Energy Sector Levies Act, 2025 (Act 1135) — Schedule (types of levies)
heading: Energy Sector Levies Act, 2025 (Act 1135) — Schedule (types of levies)

metadata.publisher: Ghana Publishing Company Limited (Assembly Press), Accra
metadata.source_authority: Parliament of the Republic of Ghana
metadata.source_note: Energy Sector Levies Act, 2025 (Act 1135) from the Parliament of Ghana DSpace ORIGINAL bitstream, downloaded 2026-07-07. Repeals the Energy Sector Levies Act, 2015 (Act 899). The Schedule (print pages 5-7) sets the Energy Sector Shortfall and Debt Repayment Levy (petrol GHp 95/l, diesel 93, naphtha 95, fuel oil 4, marine gas oil 3, LPG 73 GHp/kg), the Road Fund Levy (GHp 48/l on petrol, diesel and naphtha), the Energy Fund Levy (GHp 1/l + 1 GHp/kg kerosene) and the Public Lighting Levy (three per cent per kWh). The second row is substituted by Act 1141 and rows restated by Act 1145 (captured below); cite the current amounts from those provisions.
metadata.title: Energy Sector Levies Act, 2025 (Act 1135) — Schedule (types of levies)

body: Types of Levies (sections 1, 2, 5(a) and 5(b)) LEVY RATE PURPOSE COLLECTING AGENCY PAYMENT INFO Energy Sector Shortfall and Debt Repayment Levy (a) GHp 95 per litre on petrol, (b) GHp 93 per litre on diesel (c) GHp 95 per litre on naphtha (d) GHp 4 per litre on fuel oil (e) GHp 3 per litre on marine gas oil (0 GHp 73 per kilogramme on liquefied petroleum gas For the payment of energy sector shortfalls and energy sector legacy debts. Ghana Revenue Authority Energy Sector Support Account Road Fund Levy GHp 48 per litre on petrol, diesel and naphtha To support the maintenance of roads. Ghana Revenue Authority Road Fund Levy Account Energy Fund Levy (a) GHp 1 per litre on petrol, diesel, fuel oil and naphtha To support the activities of the Energy Commission. Ghana Revenue Authority Energy Fund Levy Account 5 Act 1135 Energy Sector Levies Act, 2025 (b) GHp 1 per kilogramme on kerosene Public Lighting Levy Three per cent per kWh of electricity charged on all categories of consumers. (a) To support the payment of electricity consumed by traffic lights, street lights, public lights and highways; (b) To support investments and maintenance of traffic lights, street lights, public lights and highways by Metropolitan, Municipal and District Assemblies; and (c) To cater for replacement of street lights destroyed by hit and run motor vehicles. (a) Electricity Company of Ghana Limited; (b) Northern Electricity Distribution Company Limited; (c) Volta River Authority; and (d) other suppliers of electricity. (a) Volta River Authority is required to transfer hundred per cent to the Public Lighting Levy Account at the Bank of Ghana to be utilised by the Ministry responsible for Power in line with this Act; (b) Electricity Company of Ghana Limited and Northern Electricity Distribution Company Limited are required to transfer fifty per cent to the Public Lighting Levy Account at the Bank of Ghana to be utilised by the Ministry responsible for Power in line with this Act; and 6 Energy Sector Levies Act, 2025 Act 1135 (c)Electricity Company of Ghana Limited and Northern Electricity Distribution Company Limited shall retain fifty per cent. National Electrification Scheme Levy Two per cent per kWh of electricity charged on all categories of consumers. To provide funding support for national electrification programme to improve access to electricity. (a) Electricity Company of Ghana Limited; (b) Northern Electricity Distribution Company Limited; (c) Volta River Authority; and (d) other suppliers of electricity. The National Electrification Fund Account at the Bank of Ghana to be utilised in line with this Act. 7
